promotions.label: ML for sleep and PA in UK Biobank
promotions.reason: Directly applicable to sensor processing: statistical machine learning of physical activity and sleep from wrist-worn accelerometer data in UK Biobank. Core domain data processing for step/sleep features in the 36-state MDP (step_bin, sleep states).

promotions.label: Smartphone HAR systematic review
promotions.reason: Systematic review of smartphone-based human activity recognition methods. Directly relevant to sensor processing pipeline for extracting physical activity features from wearable/phone sensors, which is core to the step_bin MDP state.

promotions.label: HAR comprehensive study
promotions.reason: Comprehensive study of human activity recognition using accelerometers. Core methodology for processing wearable sensor data to derive physical activity metrics needed for the JITAI MDP.

promotions.label: Role of features in HAR
promotions.reason: Investigates feature engineering for human activity recognition from wearables. Directly relevant to designing sensor processing features for the step_bin and sleep states in the factored MDP.

promotions.label: ConvLSTM for HAR
promotions.reason: Deep convolutional and LSTM architecture for multimodal human activity recognition. Relevant deep learning methodology for processing wearable sensor streams to derive activity features for the RL state space.

promotions.label: Multi-task SSL for HAR
promotions.reason: Multi-task self-supervised learning for human activity detection from wearables. Directly relevant to the project's next step of developing sensor foundation models (NormWear 768-dim embeddings) using self-supervised learning on wearable data.

promotions.label: Masked reconstruction for HAR
promotions.reason: Masked reconstruction based self-supervision for human activity recognition. Relevant SSL methodology applicable to sensor FM pre-training (next step: sensor foundation models).

promotions.label: Contrastive learning for HAR
promotions.reason: Explores contrastive learning for human activity recognition. Directly relevant methodology for sensor FM development, as contrastive learning is a key technique for learning representations from unlabeled wearable time series.

promotions.label: CPC for HAR (variant)
promotions.reason: Contrastive predictive coding for human activity recognition. Relevant SSL approach for wearable sensor representation learning, applicable to the sensor FM pipeline for extracting features from accelerometer data.

promotions.label: Contrastive learning for clinical outcomes
promotions.reason: Evaluates contrastive learning on wearable time series for clinical outcomes. Directly applicable to the project's sensor FM development path, combining SSL methods with clinical wearable data.

promotions.label: SSL for HAR state
promotions.reason: Assesses the state of self-supervised HAR using wearables. Provides a comprehensive overview of SSL techniques for wearable sensor data, directly relevant to designing the sensor FM pipeline.

promotions.label: Step detection for UK Biobank
promotions.reason: ML wrist-worn step detection algorithm. Directly core methodology: step counting from wrist accelerometers is essential for the step_bin state variable in the 36-state MDP and for evaluating physical activity interventions.

promotions.label: SSL for sleep and mortality
promotions.reason: Self-supervised learning of accelerometer data for sleep assessment. Directly relevant: sleep is a key state variable in the factored MDP (sleep feature), and SSL methods for extracting it from wearables align with the sensor FM roadmap.

promotions.label: SelfHAR
promotions.reason: Improves human activity recognition through self-training with unlabeled data. Relevant semi-supervised methodology for sensor processing, applicable when labeled activity data is limited — a practical concern for the project's sensor pipeline.
